display.purpose: Hero commands and major conversion statements.

body.purpose: Project descriptions, pricing context, and concise value copy.

label.purpose: Commands, status markers, metadata, and technical labels.

shadows.name: ambient-glow
shadows.purpose: Priority hero or CTA energy, used sparingly.

shadows.name: panel-lift
shadows.purpose: Featured cards and active surfaces only.

motion.name: system-loop
motion.purpose: Hero command and system-node animation where it clarifies technical energy.

components.name: Primary Hire Button
components.description: Sharp yellow action button for contact, hiring, and active selection.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; min-height: 2.75rem; border: 0; border-radius: 4px; background: #ffd300; color: #000000; padding: 0 1.25rem; font-family: Manrope, Arial, sans-serif; font-weight: 900; cursor: pointer; transition: transform 250ms ease, filter 250ms ease; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { transform: translateY(-1px); filter: brightness(1.04); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid #66c7ff; outline-offset: 3px; }

components.name: Icon Ghost Button
components.description: Transparent square icon control for search, menu, and utility actions.
components.html: <button class="ds-icon-button" aria-label="Search"><svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" width="22" height="22" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2"><circle cx="11" cy="11" r="7"></circle><path d="m20 20-3.5-3.5"></path></svg></button>
components.css: .ds-icon-button { display: grid; width: 2.8rem; height: 2.8rem; place-items: center; border: 0; border-radius: 4px; background: transparent; color: #fffdf6; cursor: pointer; transition: background 250ms ease, color 250ms ease; } .ds-icon-button:hover { background: rgba(255,255,255,0.08); color: #ffd300; } .ds-icon-button:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid #ffd300; outline-offset: 3px; }

components.name: Search Field
components.description: Dark bordered search field for filtering project evidence.
components.css: .ds-search { display: grid; gap: 0.5rem; color: #cfd2df; font-family: Manrope, Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 0.9rem; font-weight: 800; } .ds-search input { min-height: 3.4rem; border: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.22); border-radius: 8px; background: #12162b; color: #fffdf6; padding: 1rem 1.15rem; font: inherit; } .ds-search input::placeholder { color: #cfd2df; } .ds-search input:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid #ffd300; outline-offset: 3px; }

components.name: Project Proof Card
components.description: Dark proof surface for a project screenshot, outcome, and stack.
components.html:
Featured case study

Business system launch

Real project outcomes, technical stack, and proof should carry the persuasion.
components.css: .ds-project-card { border: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.12); border-radius: 8px; background: linear-gradient(135deg, #050505, #12162b); color: #fffdf6; padding: 1.5rem; font-family: Manrope, Arial, sans-serif; } .ds-project-card span { color: #ffd300; font-family: 'Roboto Mono', monospace; font-size: 0.78rem; font-weight: 900; } .ds-project-card h3 { margin: 1rem 0 0.5rem; font-size: 1.45rem; line-height: 1.1; } .ds-project-card p { margin: 0; color: #cfd2df; line-height: 1.7; }

components.name: Sticky Portfolio Nav
components.description: Dark command bar with concise links and a primary hire action.
components.css: .ds-nav { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 1.25rem; min-height: 5.4rem; border-bottom: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.12); background: rgba(16,19,37,0.96); color: #fffdf6; padding: 0 2rem; font-family: Manrope, Arial, sans-serif; } .ds-nav strong { display: grid; width: 1.8rem; height: 1.8rem; place-items: center; border: 3px solid #fffdf6; font-family: 'Roboto Mono', monospace; } .ds-nav a { font-weight: 800; } .ds-nav button { margin-left: auto; min-height: 2.75rem; border: 0; border-radius: 4px; background: #ffd300; color: #000000; padding: 0 1.25rem; font-weight: 900; }

narrative.overview: The system should feel like a high-contrast developer command center built for selling real product capability: dark surfaces, sharp yellow action, mono labels, proof-heavy project framing, and animated technical structures that point back to business outcomes. It is brand-first, but the brand is not abstract decoration; every console, orbit, filter, and pricing block should make Ntsinzi feel more capable to hire.
keyCharacteristics: High-contrast dark canvas with yellow as the decisive action color.
Mono labels and command-like copy for technical credibility.
Real screenshots and project outcomes as the primary trust layer.
Compact, rectilinear UI with small radii and strong borders.
Motion should feel purposeful and system-like, never ornamental.

rules.name: The Yellow Means Action Rule
rules.body: Command Yellow is for action, selection, and decisive emphasis. Do not scatter it as decoration.

rules.name: The No Purple-Blue AI Gradient Rule
rules.body: Purple-blue gradient hero treatments are prohibited. The system uses black, blueblack, yellow, and controlled signal accents.

rules.name: The Command Label Rule
rules.body: Mono text earns its place only when it reads as a command, status, metric, or technical marker. Do not use mono for ordinary paragraphs.

rules.name: The Border Before Shadow Rule
rules.body: Prefer a crisp border and tonal shift before adding shadow. If a card needs both, the shadow must support hierarchy, not decorate.

dos: Do keep Command Yellow tied to action, active selection, and decisive emphasis.
Do lead with real shipped work, screenshots, outcomes, and pricing clarity.
Do keep dark surfaces readable with Warm White or Muted Text that meets WCAG AA.
Do use motion to clarify systems, hierarchy, or progress.
Do preserve the compact code-console signature: sharp frames, strong borders, mono labels, and high contrast.
donts: Don't use generic developer portfolio templates.
Don't use vague "modern SaaS" page structure as the default.
Don't use soft glassmorphism as the visual foundation.
Don't add decorative code noise with no business meaning.
Don't turn the portfolio into a resume pasted into cards.
Don't make the site feel like a course platform unless that metaphor is serving the portfolio narrative directly.
